label: Calendar Events
description: Economic calendar and corporate actions feed. Shows upcoming events with expected impact ratings.

l0.notes: 0.1 WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx covers all grid widgets — pass. 0.2 requiredEntitlements=["data-basic","data-pro"] — pass. 0.3 availableOn=["terminal","overview"] — pass. 0.4 No any/ts-ignore — pass. 0.5 No lib/mocks imports — pass. 0.6 Loading handled inside CalendarEventFeed via useEconomicResults/useCorporateActions isLoading guards — pass. 0.7 Empty states: "No events in range" and "No corporate actions in range" in CalendarEventFeed — pass. 0.8 Error boundary in widget-wrapper covers render errors; CalendarEventFeed sub-hooks surface loading state — pass. 0.9 No dead code — pass. NO FIXES REQUIRED.

l1.notes: 1.1 Data sourced from useEconomicResults and useCorporateActions hooks (hooks/api/use-calendar.ts) via CalendarEventFeed — no inline fetch/import in widget file — pass. 1.2 hooks/api/use-calendar.ts uses useAuth() for user-scoped queries; terminal-data-context.tsx calls useGlobalScope() for instrument/account scope — calendar data is user-scoped but not filtered by org/client/strategy (correct for an economic calendar domain) — N/A for org/client/strategy filtering. 1.3 N/A — calendar data is global macro/corporate-action data, not org-filtered. 1.4 N/A — calendar data is not client-scoped. 1.5 N/A — calendar data is not strategy-scoped. 1.6 N/A — no scope filtering applies; hook fetches all events in the time window. 1.7 No reference data in lib/mocks — pass.

findings: L0: All 9 checks pass — no fixes required. Widget is a thin wrapper delegating to CalendarEventFeed which handles all data + states.
L1: 1.1 pass. 1.2–1.6 N/A — calendar events are global macro/corporate-action data not filtered by org/client/strategy scope. 1.7 pass.
